issue_title: The results of baddbmm on CPU seem to have issues under certain conditions
surface_reasoning: Priority 1: carries `module: correctness (silent)` label — PyTorch's explicit marker for silent wrong-answer bugs. labeled `high priority`. Maintainer `high priority` label confirms the bug is important and acknowledged.

linked_prs.title: Fix addmm silent correctness on aarch64
linked_prs.abandoned_reason: Author (malfet, CONTRIBUTOR) force-merged via '@pytorchbot merge -f' but GitHub API reports merged=false on main. Author subsequently reopened the issue commenting 'Re-opening the issue for a bit more involved fix (as current API is brittle), but will cherry-pick minimal fix into 2.5' — indicating the main-branch fix was intentionally deferred in favor of a more robust approach. CONTRIBUTOR swolchok had APPROVED the PR. Cherry-picked to release/2.5 as PR #137208.
linked_prs.reviews_summary: CONTRIBUTOR swolchok submitted an APPROVED review. Author malfet issued '@pytorchbot merge -f' but the fix was intentionally not landed on main. The quick fix was cherry-picked to release/2.5 (PR #137208, merged 2024-10-02).

linked_prs.title: Fix addmm silent correctness on aarch64
linked_prs.reviews_summary: Cherry-pick of PR #136371 onto release/2.5. CONTRIBUTOR kit1980 approved. Merged 2024-10-02. This only fixed release/2.5, not main branch.

maintainer_signals.author: bdhirsh
maintainer_signals.quote: I can repro on a recent main commit too

confidence_justification: Start: 0.50. Step 1: 'module: correctness (silent)' label — PyTorch's highest-priority bug category, silent wrong-answer bug → +0.25. Running total: 0.75. Step 2: Self-contained repro script in issue body — complete Python code using torch.baddbmm with specific tensor shapes, demonstrates that baddbmm results are identical for different alpha values (0.1 vs 0.2) when they should differ → +0.15. Running total: 0.90. Step 3: 'triaged' label present → +0.10. Running total: 1.00. Step 4: fix_scope = single_function (identified as the fast gemmv dispatch path in aarch64 BLAS, regression introduced by PR #127478) → +0.10. Running total: 1.10. Step 5: COLLABORATOR bdhirsh confirmed reproduction on main (scoped_bug signal). Step 6: PR #136371 exists with approved review — a contributor identified root cause, wrote a fix, got it approved, but deferred main-branch landing to write a better approach. Author commented 'Re-opening the issue for a bit more involved fix' — confirming a fix is needed. This is a revive_abandoned_pr scenario. No blocking signals. No already_fixed blocker (main branch still needs fix). Step 7: Issue open since Sep 2024, ~1.5 years — mild staleness (+0.05 for open 2+ years does not yet apply at 1.5y). Step 8: No negative adjustments. Clamp to 1.0. Final: 1.00.
ground_truth_delta: Very similar to positive_71774: both are silent correctness bugs (module: correctness (silent)), both have self-contained repro scripts, both have COLLABORATOR confirmation, and both have approved-but-unmerged fix PRs on main. Key differences: (1) issue 136299 is newer (~1.5 years vs 4+ years), reducing staleness risk; (2) the root cause was explicitly identified by the author as an aarch64-specific regression from a specific PR; (3) a partial fix DID land on release/2.5 via cherry-pick, confirming the fix approach is viable — contributor only needs to implement the more robust main-branch version that the author described as 'less brittle'.

contribution_path.title: Read the abandoned PR diff
contribution_path.description: Study PR #136371 ('Fix addmm silent correctness on aarch64') to understand the approach before writing any code. Note which files changed and the overall diff size. The PR received a COLLABORATOR APPROVED review — the fix approach is already validated.

contribution_path.title: Reproduce the bug on current main
contribution_path.description: Clone pytorch/pytorch main and run the minimal repro from issue #136299. Confirm the bug still exists before investing time in the fix.

contribution_path.title: Apply the fix on current main
contribution_path.description: Adapt the fix from PR #136371 to current main. PR #137208 (the cherry-pick) merged successfully onto release/2.5, confirming the approach is correct — apply the same logic to main. Compare the two diffs to understand what the release backport changed.
contribution_path.warning: pytorchbot shows merged=false for PR #136371 despite '@pytorchbot merge -f' being issued — this is a GitHub API race condition, not a rejection. The fix logic itself was accepted.

contribution_path.title: Run tests and add a regression test
contribution_path.description: Run `python test/test_linalg.py -k baddbmm`. PR #136371 added test cases — include them in the new PR and verify they pass on main.

contribution_path.title: Open a new PR
contribution_path.description: Submit a PR with 'Fixes #136299' and 'Revives #136371 and #137208' in the body. Tag @bdhirsh for review — they already know the issue and the fix approach.
